key_points: Public facilities are essential services provided mainly by the government for the welfare of citizens
These facilities include water supply, sanitation, electricity, healthcare, education and public transport
Access to public facilities is important for maintaining health, safety and quality of life
The government collects taxes from citizens to fund and maintain public facilities
Equal access to public facilities helps reduce social inequality
Sometimes private companies also provide services, but the government must regulate them to ensure fairness
examples: Municipal corporations supply clean drinking water to households in cities
Government hospitals provide affordable healthcare services
Public buses and trains help people travel for work and education
Electricity boards provide power for homes and industries
formulas: Public Facilities → Government services + Public welfare
Social equality → Equal access to services
Public services funding → Taxes collected by government
exam_tips: Explain the meaning of public facilities clearly
Write examples such as water supply, sanitation and hospitals
Mention how the government uses taxes to provide these facilities
Explain why equal access to public facilities is important
